title: oci: keep exposed ports busy and leak the fd into conmon
body:
Bind all the specified TCP and UDP ports so that another process
cannot reuse them.  The fd of the listener is then leaked into conmon
so that the socket is kept busy until the container exits.

Closes: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/issues/210

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
there might be a race condition where conmon notifies us that the container exited but the ports are still busy and when we restart the container we might find them used.  I think it is very difficult to hit as the cleanup phase of conmon is much faster than setting up again the container to run, but still it is possible.  I've opened a PR here for conmon:

https://github.com/kubernetes-incubator/cri-o/pull/1698
Could you do a test where we tell a container to bind to a port and then in the next line try to bind to the port using ncat?
added a test
@mheon PTAL

LGTM. Let's see if I can trigger a merge over email...
@rh-atomic-bot r+

Error copying image to the remote destination: Error checking for blob sha256:73046094a9b835e443af1a9d736fcfc11a994107500e474d0abf399499ed280c at destination: pinging docker registry returned: Get http://localhost:5000/v2/: read tcp [::1]:45250->[::1]:5000: read: connection reset by peer

@giuseppe We can't even merge a docs-only change, so I doubt it's your code

> I wonder if it fails because of the new code, it hangs on reading from the registry:

It took a while but finally I reproduced the error here.  It happens when trying to resolve `localhost` instead of `127.0.0.1`.  If I bind the port both on IPv4 and IPv6, then when connecting on IPv6, the connection hangs on our socket.  The connection to the registry seems to happen on IPv6.

I've changed the PR to bind only IPv4 now, let's see if it makes any difference in the CI.

This is still a problem if someone tries to use the port on IPv6 and hijack connections, but it should be solved in CNI.  /cc @dcbw

> @giuseppe We can't even merge a docs-only change, so I doubt it's your code

yes the CI seems still broken, but this PR is even more broken than the other ones :-)
@mheon finally tests are passing \o/ It was because of the IPv4 vs IPv6 issue

Alright, let's give this a shot...
@rh-atomic-bot r+
